skill.name: Azure Key Vault Certificates SDK for Rust
skill.description: A Rust SDK for managing certificates in Azure Key Vault. This skill provides Claude with the knowledge to help developers integrate Azure Key Vault certificate operations into their Rust applications, including creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting certificates, as well as managing certificate policies and versions.
skill.summary: Rust SDK for Azure Key Vault certificate management with operations for create, get, update, delete, and policy management.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing documentation for the Azure Key Vault Certificates Rust SDK. The static analysis scanned 0 files and detected no security issues. The skill provides educational content about Azure certificate management APIs without any executable code, network requests, or file system access. No risk factors detected.

content.user_title: Manage Azure Key Vault Certificates in Rust
content.value_statement: Developers building Rust applications need secure certificate management for Azure Key Vault but lack guidance on implementing the SDK. This skill provides Claude with comprehensive knowledge of the Azure Key Vault Certificates Rust SDK to help developers integrate certificate operations.
seo_keywords: Azure Key Vault certificates Rust SDK
actual_capabilities: Generate Rust code for creating new certificates in Azure Key Vault with custom policies
Generate Rust code for retrieving and parsing certificates from Azure Key Vault
Generate Rust code for updating and deleting certificates and their versions
Generate Rust code for managing certificate policies including issuance parameters
Generate Rust code for handling certificate imports and backups
Generate Rust code for working with certificate operations and pending requests
limitations: Does not provide actual Azure authentication credentials or connections
Cannot execute any operations against real Azure Key Vault instances
Only provides code examples and documentation guidance, not runtime execution
Requires users to have their own Azure subscription and Key Vault setup

use_cases.title: Rust Backend Developer
use_cases.description: A backend developer building a Rust service that needs to store and retrieve TLS certificates from Azure Key Vault for HTTPS endpoints.
use_cases.target_user: Rust backend developers building secure web services

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer
use_cases.description: A DevOps engineer creating infrastructure automation scripts in Rust to manage certificate lifecycle including rotation and renewal.

use_cases.title: Security Engineer
use_cases.description: A security engineer implementing certificate management policies and audit trails for Azure-based applications.

prompt_templates.title: Create a new certificate
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create a new certificate in Azure Key Vault using the Rust SDK with a custom certificate policy that includes subject name, validity period, and key usage parameters.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating code for new certificate creation with policy configuration

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve and parse certificate
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Rust code to retrieve a certificate from Azure Key Vault and extract the public and private key components for use in an HTTPS server.
prompt_templates.scenario: Fetching certificates for use in TLS/HTTPS applications

prompt_templates.title: Update certificate policy
prompt_templates.prompt: Show how to update the certificate policy for an existing certificate in Azure Key Vault to change the validity period and key usage.
prompt_templates.scenario: Modifying certificate policies for existing certificates

prompt_templates.title: Handle certificate operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Write code to check the status of a pending certificate operation in Azure Key Vault and retrieve the completed certificate once ready.

output_examples.input: Create a certificate with RSA key type and 2-year validity
output_examples.output: The skill generates Rust code using the azure_identity crate for authentication and the azure_security_keyvault_certificates crate for operations. The code includes: CertificatePolicy builder with KeyProperties (RSA, 2048-bit), LifetimeAction for auto-renewal, and SecretProperties for storage. The example shows error handling with azure_core::errors::Error and proper async/await patterns.

The skill provides code showing CertificateImportOperation with Pfx format, password handling for decryption, and the import method with the key vault client. It demonstrates handling the imported certificate's ID and version for later retrieval.

best_practices: Always use managed identities or service principals with least-privilege access for Azure authentication in production environments
Implement proper error handling for certificate operations including network failures and Azure service errors
Use certificate versions to track changes and implement proper rollback procedures for certificate rotation
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode Azure Key Vault URLs or credentials in source code; use environment variables or Azure Key Vault references
Do not ignore certificate expiration; implement automated monitoring and renewal workflows
Do not share private keys across multiple certificates; each certificate should have its own unique key material

faq.question: What authentication methods does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill covers Azure Active Directory authentication including service principals, managed identities, and Azure CLI credentials through the azure_identity crate.

faq.question: Can this skill help with certificate rotation?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides examples for retrieving certificate versions, implementing lifetime actions, and managing the rotation workflow for certificates with new key material.

faq.question: Does this skill support HSM-backed certificates?
faq.answer: The skill includes guidance on using Azure Key Vault Premium tier with HSM-protected keys, showing how to specify the KeyType for HSM key operations.

faq.question: How do I handle certificate passwords securely?
faq.answer: The skill demonstrates passing certificate passwords as SecureString and recommends using Azure Key Vault secrets to store password values rather than hardcoding them.

faq.question: Can this skill generate code for self-signed certificates?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill shows how to create self-signed certificates using the CertificatePolicy with IssuancePolicy for self-issuance parameters.

faq.question: What error handling patterns does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: The skill covers azure_core::errors::Error handling, retry logic for transient failures, and proper logging for certificate operations debugging.
